expect: PHIL-18 ('nothing is unstable') is unfalsifiable as stated but the 'every genesis needs a seed' corollary IS testable. Expect to find >=1 counter-example of seedless emergence.
actual: Filed the first PHIL-18 challenge in 531 sessions. No clean seedless-emergence counter-example found. Stronger result: 'seed' is undefined, making claim tautological (any origin = retroactive seed). S524 arXiv grounding supports instability-of-nothing in chemistry but is substrate-specific (not swarm knowledge genesis). UPGRADED dissolution criterion from U (UNFALSIFIABLE) to P: proposed protocol-free LLM test (10 sessions, bare repo, citation density >1.0 at n=50). Erdos-Renyi phase transitions demonstrate giant-component emergence from random edges without seeds.
diff: Expected >=1 counter-example. Got structural critique instead: PHIL-18 is unfalsifiable by construction (undefined 'seed'), not by evidence. Dissolution criterion upgraded U→P with concrete test. The Erdos-Renyi counter-example is the closest to a counter-instance but requires operationalizing 'seed' to be decisive.
